title: silene pteroneura
intro: Silene pteroneura, also known as mountain catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and soothing skin irritations due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Silene pteroneura can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
